contract_claim: Comment says `javac -version` prints to stdout (JDK 9+). In reality, `javac -version` prints to stderr in many JDK versions (including some JDK 9+ builds). The stdio config captures both stdout and pipe (`['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']`), meaning stderr IS captured, so the code actually works — but the comment's claim that output goes to stdout is misleading.
observed_behavior: Comment says `javac -version` prints to stdout (JDK 9+). In reality, `javac -version` prints to stderr in many JDK versions (including some JDK 9+ builds). The stdio config captures both stdout and pipe (`['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']`), meaning stderr IS captured, so the code actually works — but the comment's claim that output goes to stdout is misleading.
